title: ConocoPhillips (COP) to Pay $914,000 Fine Over Alaska Gas Leak
text: The Alaska Oil and Gas Conservation Commission accuses ConocoPhillips (COP) of violating several state law regulations which led to the blunder that caused the leak.

title: 3 Energy Stocks That Just Have No More Hope Left
text: In early April, prominent Wall St analysts predicted that oil prices could go beyond $100 per barrel as OPEC+ cut output while demand was supposed to surge due to China's reopening. However, this prediction has failed to come to fruition, as oil prices have barely budged beyond $70 a barrel, down from over $80 in April.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Is a Trending Stock: Facts to Know Before Betting on It
text: Zacks.com users have recently been watching ConocoPhillips (COP) quite a bit. Thus, it is worth knowing the facts that could determine the stock's prospects.

title: 3 Energy Stocks That Have Big Investors Running for the Exit
text: The party in energy stocks looks to be over. In 2022, oil and natural gas stocks were the only market segment that saw gains, as the price of crude oil surged to $122 per barrel.

title: Here's Why Hold Strategy is Apt for ConocoPhillips (COP) Now
text: Favorable oil price is aiding ConocoPhillips' (COP) bottom line. However, increasing production and operating expenses are hurting it.

title: 500 Top Dividend Dogs
text: The June/July 2023 edition of Fortune Magazine revealed 500 biggest U.S. companies by revenue. To sniff out the best dividend buys, I focused on 68 industry leaders using YCharts 6/22/23 data. Those 68 industry leaders were segmented into 21 business sectors ranging (alphabetically) from apparel to transportation. 58 dividend yielding stocks highlighted those 68 industry leaders and ranged 0.17% to 9.71% in annual yield and ranged -48.62% to 44.50% in broker-estimated one-year price-target-upsides by YCharts 6/22/23 reckoning.

title: ConocoPhillips to Hold Second-Quarter Earnings Conference Call on Thursday, Aug 3
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--ConocoPhillips will host a conference call webcast on Thursday, Aug. 3, 2023, at 12:00 p.m. ET to discuss 2Q 2023 financial and operating results.

title: 3 Best Stocks to Sell Before the Next Recession
text: The bears have been calling for a recession since mid-2021, citing the unnatural shift post-pandemic in monetary policy and the lack of production to support the stimulus-induced demand. If you go by the book, much of that has already come true.

title: 2 Oil Stocks to Buy Even If Oil Prices Keep Slumping
text: Chevron can generate enough cash at $60 oil to grow its business and dividend. ConocoPhillips' free cash flow forecast positions it to send a boatload of cash back to shareholders even if oil averages $60 a barrel.

title: These 3 Bakken Stocks are Well Poised to Gain: Here's Why
text: Favorable oil price is backing modestly higher production volumes in Bakken. Companies that are thus well poised to gain are Hess (HES), Marathon Oil (MRO) and ConocoPhillips (COP).

title: Investors Heavily Search ConocoPhillips (COP): Here is What You Need to Know
text: Zacks.com users have recently been watching ConocoPhillips (COP) quite a bit. Thus, it is worth knowing the facts that could determine the stock's prospects.

title: 4 Integrated US Oil Stocks Set to Escape Industry Weakness
text: From upstream activities to refining, prospects for companies are not at all rosy now, dampening the outlook for the Zacks Oil & Gas US Integrated industry. COP, HES, WHD and PHX will likely survive the business challenges.

title: Investors Seeking Income Should Check Out ConocoPhillips
text: ConocoPhillips stock is a stellar income generation idea as past deleveraging activities have set the firm up nicely for the future. Its high-quality asset base is home to vast oil and natural gas resources, which should enable ConocoPhillips to steadily grow its output over the long haul. ConocoPhillips is a free cash flow generating cow that's incredibly shareholder friendly.

title: 11 Buyable Inflation-Busting June Dividend Stocks
text: March-April 2022, UBS, Kiplinger, and Barron's analysts/reporters listed five sets of stocks sorted as “Inflation Busters.” Those five-articles revealed 48 equities, with 43 paying-dividends. This over one-year-old list continues to beat-back new-strains-of-inflation.

title: 3 High-Risk Oil Stocks to Sell in June Before They Crash and Burn
text: When it comes to investing in the oil sector, it's crucial to assess the market landscape. Sometimes, selling oil stocks may be prudent, especially when dealing with high-risk and potentially overvalued options.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Commences Alaska's Nuna Oilfield Project
text: ConocoPhillips (COP) expects drilling activities to start in late 2024, with the first oil anticipated by early 2025.

title: Chevron, Exxon, Oil Stocks Rise After Saudi Arabia Pledges Production Cut at OPEC+ Summit
text: Jefferies analysts pose the question of whether OPEC+ cutting barrels is “a good or bad indicator” for oil markets and oil stocks. The answer is a bit mixed.

title: High Yield Stock Watchlist: June 2023
text: There are 20 stocks on my high yield watchlist for June 2023. The majority of the stocks on my watchlist are undervalued based on dividend yield theory. An equally weighted portfolio of these stocks underperformed the Vanguard High Yield Dividend ETF by about 1.5% during the month of May.

title: 7 Sorry Energy Stocks to Sell in June Before It's Too Late
text: The landscape looks quite grim as we assess underperforming energy stocks. Despite the anticipation that China's economic reopening would rekindle some lost vigor, many energy stocks are in a troubling position.
